breastfeedingAt6Weeks.note: Percentage of babies breastfed at their 6-8 week review, England

breastfeedingAt6Months.note: Estimated percentage of babies receiving any breastmilk at 6 months, England

formulaCostMonthly.note: Average monthly cost of infant formula per baby (first stage), England

sources.name: NHS Digital
sources.dataset: Child Health Information Service — breastfeeding at 6-8 weeks

sources.name: UNICEF UK Baby Friendly Initiative
sources.dataset: UK breastfeeding rates and international comparisons

sources.name: Which? / The Grocer
sources.dataset: Infant formula price tracking, UK retailers

metadata.methodology: Breastfeeding at 6-8 weeks from CHIS returns submitted by NHS trusts to NHS Digital — covers health visitor contacts in England. Six-month breastfeeding rates estimated from Infant Feeding Survey (last full survey 2010) updated with modelling from academic sources. Formula costs from retail price tracking of leading brands across major UK supermarkets.
knownIssues: Full Infant Feeding Survey last conducted in 2010 — six-month figures are estimates
2020 figures affected by COVID-19 disruption to health visitor services
Formula cost tracking covers standard first-stage formula only — specialist formulas considerably more expensive
